title: The Three Transformer Families
concept: Transformers split into: Encoder-only (BERT), Decoder-only (GPT), and Encoder-Decoder (T5/BART), matching different task needs.

exercises.prompt: Into how many main families is the Transformer architecture split?
exercises.explain: Transformers are divided into three main families (Encoder-only, Decoder-only, and Encoder-Decoder) to handle different tasks efficiently.

cards.title: Meet the Families
cards.body: Not all AI brains work the same way! Just like humans have different strengths, the famous Transformer AI architecture is split into three unique families, each built for a different kind of task. Let's meet them!

cards.title: The Three Families
cards.body:
Transformers come in three specialized families:

1. **Encoder-only** (like BERT): Great at reading and understanding text.
2. **Decoder-only** (like GPT): Great at writing and generating new text.
3. **Encoder-Decoder** (like T5): Great at translating or converting text from one form to another.

cards.title: The Kitchen Tools Analogy
cards.body:
Think of Transformers like tools in a kitchen:

• Encoder-only (BERT) is like a magnifying glass. It inspects what's already there to help you understand it.
• Decoder-only (GPT) is like a pen. It starts with a blank page and writes out new text.
• Encoder-Decoder (T5) is like a language translator. It reads a recipe in French and writes it out in English.

cards.title: Choosing the Right Tool
cards.body:
Here is how you would choose a model for different school projects:

• Analyzing the mood of book reviews? Use **BERT** (Encoder-only) to read and classify.
• Writing a creative story about robots? Use **GPT** (Decoder-only) to generate the text.
• Translating homework from Spanish to English? Use **T5** (Encoder-Decoder) to translate.

cards.title: One Size Doesn't Fit All
cards.body:
**Myth:** One giant Transformer model is the best at absolutely everything.
**Reality:** A Decoder-only model is great at writing, but an Encoder-only model is often much faster and more accurate at analyzing existing text. Using the right family saves time and energy!

cards.title: Comparing Structures
cards.body: See how the structure changes based on the goal: reading, writing, or translating.

left.text: Reads the whole sentence at once to understand its deep meaning.

right.text: Generates text one word at a time, looking only at what was written before.

exercises.prompt: If you want to build a system that reads customer emails and automatically tags them as 'Happy' or 'Angry', which Transformer family is the best fit?
exercises.explain: BERT is an Encoder-only model, making it excellent for searching, classifying, and understanding the meaning of text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Encoder-Decoder models are commonly used for translating languages.
exercises.explain: Encoder-Decoder models are designed to take an input sequence (like French text) and convert it into a different output sequence (like English text).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing family type for GPT.
exercises.sentence: GPT is a famous example of a ________ model.
exercises.explain: GPT belongs to the Decoder-only family, which excels at writing and completing sentences one word at a time.

exercises.prompt: Which of these models is specifically designed to write creative stories from scratch?
exercises.explain: GPT (a Decoder-only model) is designed to generate new content word by word. BERT is for analysis, and T5 is for sequence-to-sequence translation.

exercises.prompt: Match each of the three models to their correct Transformer family!

options.text: GPT is an Encoder, BERT is a Decoder, T5 is a Hybrid
GPT, BERT, and T5 are all Decoder-only models
BERT is Encoder-only, GPT is Decoder-only, and T5 is Encoder-Decoder
BERT, GPT, and T5 are all Encoder-only models

exercises.explain: GPT is a Decoder-only model designed to write next words. BERT is an Encoder-only model designed to understand context. T5 is an Encoder-Decoder model built for converting one sequence into another (like translation).
